questions.question: Which CSS property applies a mask to an element?
questions.explanation: The mask property (and its sub-properties) applies masking to elements.

questions.question: What color in a mask image reveals the element?
questions.explanation: White or opaque areas in a mask reveal the element; black/transparent areas hide it.

questions.question: Which mask-mode uses color brightness to determine visibility?
questions.explanation: luminance mode uses the brightness of colors to determine which parts are visible.

questions.question: How do you reference an SVG <mask> element in CSS?
questions.explanation: SVG masks are referenced using the URL function: mask: url(#myMask);

questions.question: Which technique creates gradient text by cutting text out of a background?
questions.explanation: background-clip: text with -webkit-text-fill-color: transparent reveals the background through text characters.

questions.question: What does mask-composite: subtract do?
questions.explanation: mask-composite: subtract removes the areas of the second mask from the first mask.

questions.question: Which property controls how a mask image repeats?
questions.explanation: mask-repeat controls tiling behavior, similar to background-repeat.

questions.question: Which browser prefix is often needed for mask properties?
questions.explanation: Many mask properties require the -webkit- prefix for broad browser support.

questions.question: What does mask-size: cover do?
options: Scales mask to cover the entire element
questions.explanation: mask-size: cover scales the mask image to cover the entire element, potentially cropping it.

questions.question: Which masking approach creates a smooth fade-out effect on an image?
questions.explanation: A linear-gradient mask from black to transparent creates a smooth fade-out effect.

questions.question: What is the key difference between mask and clip-path?
options: mask uses alpha/luminance for soft edges; clip-path creates hard edges
clip-path uses alpha; mask creates hard edges
questions.explanation: mask uses alpha/luminance for soft edges and partial transparency; clip-path creates hard-edged clipping only.

questions.question: How does mask-mode: alpha differ from mask-mode: luminance?
options: alpha uses transparency channel; luminance uses color brightness
alpha uses grayscale; luminance uses alpha
questions.explanation: alpha mode uses the mask's alpha/transparency channel; luminance mode uses the brightness of colors.

questions.question: What is the mask shorthand property order?
options: mask: image repeat position / size clip
questions.explanation: The mask shorthand follows: mask: image repeat position / size clip similar to background shorthand.

questions.question: Can mask animations be performance-friendly?
options: Yes, especially when using transform and opacity with masks
questions.explanation: Masks combined with GPU-accelerated properties like transform and opacity can animate performantly.

questions.question: What happens when you apply a partially transparent mask?
options: Element is partially revealed with matching opacity
questions.explanation: A partially transparent mask (e.g., 50% gray) reveals the element at that opacity level.

questions.question: Which value of mask-clip defines the area affected by the mask?
questions.explanation: mask-clip accepts border-box, padding-box, content-box, and margin-box to define the mask area.

questions.question: How does CSS masking differ from opacity?
options: opacity affects the whole element uniformly; masks can selectively reveal/hide per-pixel
questions.explanation: opacity uniformly fades the entire element; masks can create complex patterns of visibility across the element.

questions.question: What is the purpose of mask-position?
options: Positions the mask image within the element
questions.explanation: mask-position positions the mask image relative to the element, similar to background-position.

questions.question: Which modern CSS feature is mask-border used for?
options: Creating image-based border masks that tile around the border
questions.explanation: mask-border slices a mask image into 9 parts and applies them to the border edges and corners.

questions.question: What does mask-composite: add do?
options: Combines multiple masks by adding their visible areas
questions.explanation: mask-composite: add combines multiple mask layers so the visible areas of all masks are shown.
